symptoms.description: engine won't start but is being turned over
squeaking noise when driving over bumps
crackling sound when moving with steering wheel turned
clicking sound when trying to start the engine and the starter does not turn
squealing sound when engine is on
ticking sound when engine is on
car able to roll with parking brake engaged
brake pedal can travel too far
clutch pedal can travel too far
turbocharger is making a whistling noise
smell of exhaust gases inside the car
car is too bouncy when driving over bumps
steering wheel is difficult to turn
car pulling to one side while moving
no cold air from climate control
no hot air from climate control
rpm's are increasing but no acceleration
battery light is on when engine is on

problems.name: Worn brake pads
diagnostic_steps: Inspect brake pad thickness visually then using a gauge if needed
Check rotor surface for scoring or grooves
Test brake pedal feel and travel distance
Check for uneven wear between left and right pads
Replace the brake pads if needed

problems.name: Warped front brake rotors
diagnostic_steps: Check the front brake rotors visually
Check the runout of the rotors
Replace the rotors if needed

problems.name: Warped rear brake rotors
diagnostic_steps: Check the front brake rotors visually
Check the runout of the rotors
Replace the rotors if needed

problems.name: Seized brake caliper piston/slide pins
diagnostic_steps: Remove the calipers
Check if the pistons and slide pins can me moved
Replace/regrease the necessary parts

problems.name: Fuel/compression/spark problems
diagnostic_steps: Check if each cylinder has the correct compression pressure
Check that the correct fuel pressure is present
Check that the injector opening signal is present
If it is a gasoline engine, check for spark

problems.name: Worn/dry suspension bushings
diagnostic_steps: Check the bushings visually
If tears are present or the bushing has moved out of place or excessive movement is present - replace it
If the bushings are too dry - spray them with silicon grease

problems.name: Blown head gasket
diagnostic_steps: Do a combustion gas test
If combustion gases are indeed present in the cooling system - replace the headgasket

problems.name: Alternator has stopped working
diagnostic_steps: Check the battery voltage with the engine on
If the voltage is below 13.5V, and the serpentine belt is ok alternator repair/replacement is needed

problems.name: Leak in the exhaust system
diagnostic_steps: Visually check the exhaust system
Repair/replace any components if needed

problems.name: Leak in the intake system
diagnostic_steps: Visually check the exhaust system
If needed, connect a smoke machine to find less visible/smaller leaks
Repair/replace any components if needed

problems.name: Worn/degraded tyres
diagnostic_steps: Visually check the tyres
Check tread wear using calipers/gauge
Check that the tyres are not older than 5 years
Replace the tyres if needed

problems.name: Worn wheel bearing
diagnostic_steps: Lift the car and check the wheels for non-rotational movement
Turn the wheels, and listen for any abnormal sounds
Replace the bearing if needed

problems.name: Worn CV joint
diagnostic_steps: Check the CV joint for radial play
Remove the boot from the CV joint and check for wear
If needed replace and regrease the CV joint and boot

problems.name: Leaking fuel injector
diagnostic_steps: Check 02 values in exhaust gases
If 02 values are low - send the injectors for testing

problems.name: Discharged/bad battery
diagnostic_steps: Check the battery voltage
If the voltage is below 11V try charging it
If the voltage is still low after charging it or if the battery can be discharged too quickly - replace the battery

problems.name: Air in clutch hydraulic lines (if hydraulic)
diagnostic_steps: Bleed the clutch's hydraulic system and add brake fluid if needed
Test again
